title: 'Rulers have become killers': Mamata fumes over attack on nephew; BJP denies role, hits back
content: TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee was allegedly attacked with stones and shoes in West Bengal's Sonarpur while visiting families affected by post-poll violence. Opposition parties, including the Congress and Samajwadi Party, condemned the incident, accusing the BJP of orchestrating the attack. The BJP denied involvement, attributing the incident to local anger.

title: Nagpur man slips, dies in house; bedridden wife starves to death amid extreme heat
content: A 77-year-old retired defence employee and his bedridden wife were found dead in their locked Nagpur home. The husband likely died first after a fall, leaving his wife to succumb days later to dehydration and starvation in the extreme summer heat. Their tragic end highlights a painful and lonely death due to lack of immediate support.

title: Rajat Patidar breaks silence on India's T20 captaincy
content: Rajat Patidar has brushed aside talk of India's T20 captaincy and selection, insisting his sole focus is on helping Royal Challengers Bengaluru win a second successive IPL title. On the eve of the final against Gujarat Titans, the RCB skipper highlighted the team's culture shift towards shared responsibility and a winning mindset.

title: Abhishek Banerjee hospitalised after Sonarpur attack; Mamata reaches hospital
content: Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee faced an attack in Sonarpur. Locals reportedly threw stones and eggs at him. His aunt, Mamata Banerjee, visited him in the hospital. She accused the BJP of orchestrating the incident. Videos of the protest have emerged online. TMC leaders claim the BJP aimed to disrupt Banerjee's outreach.

title: 'Amazon would collapse if run like New York City': Jeff Bezos takes swipe Mamdani
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has endorsed New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's new government efficiency initiative, COGE. This comes after their public disagreement on taxing the wealthy. Bezos, who previously criticized city spending, now supports Mamdani's plan to make government work smarter, suggesting savings could benefit lower earners.

title: Iran war: A China link to US losing its first fighter jet in decades
content: A US F-15E Strike Eagle was reportedly shot down over Iran by a Chinese-made shoulder-launched missile, marking a significant escalation. While US officials investigate, China denies providing recent military support, though past assistance and potential radar technology transfers are noted. The incident complicates ongoing US-China relations amid peace negotiations.

title: Explained: How Nihal Sarin secured India's first-ever Esports Nations Cup chess invite
content: While Nihal and his S8UL teammate Aravindh Chithambaram have both successfully qualified for the upcoming Esports World Cup (EWC) 2026 Chess main event, Nihal stands alone as India's premier direct invitee for the country-based ENC tournament, where he will represent Team India Esports.

title: Hundreds of faculty members of California university sign open letter on maths, science papers
content: University of California faculty are urging the reinstatement of SAT/ACT math requirements for STEM applicants, citing a significant decline in student math skills since the tests were dropped. They argue current admissions practices fail to ensure readiness, forcing instructors to reteach basic math and jeopardizing the quality of STEM education.

title: ‘Throw age out of the window’: Rabada's blunt take on Sooryavanshi
content: Gujarat Titans bowler Kagiso Rabada lauded Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. The 15-year-old's IPL 2026 season was marked by fearless batting and sharp reflexes. Sooryavanshi scored 776 runs, leading the tournament charts. Rabada emphasized the youngster's maturity and attacking approach, stating age is irrelevant to his performance. Gujarat Titans secured a spot in the IPL 2026 final.

title: 'De-extincting' the Moa: The audacious bid to bring back the giant bird
content: A Texas-based firm, Colossal Biosciences, claims a breakthrough in de-extinction, developing an artificial eggshell system to potentially resurrect the extinct moa. While scientists express skepticism due to a lack of peer-reviewed data, the project is driven by ecological restoration, cultural significance for Māori, and the development of biotechnology for living endangered species.

title: Microsoft CEO's Copilot ‘confusion fix’ that left employees laughing in townhall
content: Microsoft is reportedly developing a 'super app' to consolidate its confusing array of Copilot AI tools. This unified platform aims to end customer frustration by offering a single destination for various AI assistants, including coding, chat, and collaboration features. The app, expected by summer's end, will allow seamless switching between personal and work accounts, simplifying Microsoft's AI ecosystem.

title: 'They wanted to kill me': TMC's Abhishek Banerjee attacked by mob, eggs & stones hurled - Watch
content: Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee faced an attack during a visit to Sonarpur South in West Bengal. Stones and eggs were thrown at his convoy. Banerjee was meeting families affected by post-poll violence. He alleged the BJP was behind the incident. This visit marked his first public appearance after the assembly election results.

title: Vinesh Phogat knocked out of Asian Games trials after semifinal loss to Meenakshi
content: The defeat ended Vinesh's campaign after an eventful day in which she had first secured permission to compete in the 53kg category and then battled her way into the last four.

title: ‘Extremely Severe’ alert buzzes phones as heavy rain lashes parts of Delhi, UP
content: Residents in Delhi-NCR and western Uttar Pradesh received an "Extremely Severe Alert" on their phones, warning of thunderstorms, lightning, strong winds, heavy rain, and hailstorms. The emergency notification, part of the government's cell broadcast system, advised people to stay indoors and take precautions against severe weather.

title: CUET chaos: Uncertainty & tears as students left in lurch at Noida centre
content: Students faced significant disruptions during the CUET-UG 2026 examination due to technical glitches and administrative confusion. Many were left waiting for hours at centers, with some exams not starting at all, leading to protests and questions about the NTA's operational preparedness.

title: Karnataka CLP elects DK Shivakumar as leader; CM oath ceremony on June 3
content: DK Shivakumar has been elected as the new leader of the Congress Legislature Party in Bengaluru. This decision follows Siddaramaiah's resignation as Karnataka chief minister. Shivakumar's astrologer has recommended June 3rd at 5:15 PM for his oath-taking ceremony at Lok Bhavan.

title: Jacqueline, Sukesh Chandrasekhar to face charges in Rs 200 crore money laundering case
content: The Patiala House court on Saturday directed framing of charges against Sukesh Chandrasekhar, Leena Maria Paul, Bollywood actor Jacqueline Fernandes and others in an alleged money laundering case linked with Rs. 200 crores alleged extortion from Aditi Singh.

title: ‘Brown’ trailer: Karisma Kapoor’s raw transformation leaves fans stunned — watch
content: Karisma Kapoor enthralls viewers with her compelling performance as Rita Brown in the much-anticipated series 'Brown.' Her shift from glamour to a raw, emotionally layered role has garnered widespread acclaim, highlighting a stunning evolution in her acting journey. Fans are rallying around this fearless, authentic depiction, celebrating a significant milestone for the cherished actress in the streaming realm.

title: Operation Sindoor 2.0: How the army is preparing for the next battle
content: India's Army is undergoing a significant transformation post-Operation Sindoor, shifting towards preemptive, technology-driven deterrence. New formations like Rudra Brigades and Bhairav battalions, alongside integrated drone units, are enhancing combat readiness. This modernization focuses on unmanned systems, AI, and rapid response, reflecting a decisive move from manpower-heavy tactics to a high-tech, multi-domain force.

title: Rain lashes parts of Delhi-NCR; IMD issues yellow alert
content: Delhi-NCR experienced a welcome respite from the heat as heavy rains brought down temperatures significantly on Saturday. The India Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for light rain, thunderstorms, and gusty winds, with further temperature drops anticipated.  Air quality has also seen improvement, moving into the satisfactory category.

title: Artistry vs Adrenaline: Gill owns the night, Sooryavanshi wins hearts
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless 96 once again showcased the teenager's extraordinary talent, but it was Shubman Gill's sublime century that carried Gujarat Titans into the IPL final. While Sooryavanshi provided the thrills, Gill's masterclass in timing and placement sealed a record playoff chase and a date with RCB.

title: Truck carrying Afghan refugees from Pakistan overturns, killing 22
content: A tragic road accident in eastern Afghanistan claimed at least 22 lives, including 10 children and five women, as a truck carrying returning refugees overturned. Officials reported around 36 others injured. The incident, attributed to the driver reportedly falling asleep, highlights the perilous journeys of Afghans returning from Pakistan and Iran amidst increased expulsions.

title: US cop fines woman for texting while driving - why it caused big row
content: A Florida woman, ticketed for holding a cellphone in her right hand while driving despite lacking one, had her citation dismissed. The Palm Beach County Sheriff's Office requested the dismissal due to insufficient evidence after the unusual traffic stop gained viral attention. The deputy's initial observation was contradicted by video evidence, leading to the case's resolution.

title: Train staff caught washing utensils in toilet; IRCTC gets notice - video
content: FSSAI has issued a statutory notice to IRCTC after a viral video showed catering staff washing utensils in a train toilet on the Lokmanya Tilak Terminus-Ernakulam Duronto Express. The food regulator called the act extremely unhygienic and a serious food safety concern, demanding an immediate action taken report and explanation.

title: 2 wives, domestic abuse, deadly plot: Chilling details in ex-Congress sarpanch's family murder
content: A shocking quadruple murder has rocked Ajmer, with police confirming the deaths of a former sarpanch and three family members were not accidental but a planned act. Investigators allege the man's first wife and their two children are responsible, stemming from alleged domestic abuse following his second marriage. The victims' charred bodies were found in a burnt SUV.

title: 'Even if you slit my throat ... ': TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee defiant after FIR, CID summons
content: Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee has defiantly responded to summons from the West Bengal CID, vowing not to be intimidated by investigative agencies.  He stated that even facing severe threats would not make him yield.  The summons follows a series of legal challenges and a recent visit by Kolkata Police to his residence, which was described as routine.

title: ‘Nobody wants to see another Ram’: Arun Govil speaks on backlash over Ranbir Kapoor's ‘Ramayana’
content: Veteran actor Arun Govil, who will play King Dasharath in Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana, said nobody wants to see another Rama, explaining the backlash Ranbir Kapoor faced. However, he called Ranbir a good actor. Ranbir also revealed he will play both Lord Rama and Lord Parshurama in the film releasing on Diwali 2026.

title: 80 launch pads, 3 octagon installations: 'Alarming' Chinese buildup near nuke silos
content: According to images examined by Reuters, China has built more than 80 launch pads along with three large octagon-shaped facilities near the Hami nuclear missile silo field in the country's north-west. The launch pads could be used by mobile missile launchers and air-defence systems, while the larger facilities may support communications, electronic warfare and military command functions, according to security analysts who reviewed the imagery.

title: A year after India broke Pak's offensive during Op Sindoor, Joint Air Defence Doctrine release
content: India's defence forces are enhancing aerial protection with the new Joint Air Defence Doctrine, integrating Army, Navy, and Air Force for a unified shield. This move, following successful operations and global vulnerabilities, aims to counter modern threats like drones and hypersonic weapons.  Technological advancements, including new missile systems, bolster this multi-layered defence strategy.

title: No need to leave US for green card, DHS clarifies
content: US Homeland Security has clarified that green card applicants generally don't need to leave the country during processing, easing confusion from an earlier announcement. While officers retain case-by-case discretion, especially for those with visa overstays or reliance on public aid, no major policy shift has occurred.

title: 'NEET, CBSE, SSC. And today CUET': Rahul says 'not a single exam conducted with honesty'
content: Rahul Gandhi and opposition leaders have strongly criticized the Centre following widespread technical glitches that delayed the CUET-UG 2026 exam. This incident, following similar issues with NEET, CBSE, and SSC, has fueled accusations of systemic failures in conducting national examinations fairly and efficiently, raising concerns about student futures.

title: TOSS SSC, Inter result 2026 out: Direct link to download marks memo here
content: The Telangana Open School Society (TOSS) has officially announced the SSC (Class 10) and Intermediate (Class 12) Public examination results for 2026 on its website, telanganaopenschool.org. Students who appeared for these flexible learning examinations can now access and download their marks memos by entering their hall ticket details. Candidates can check the direct link to download hall tickets here.

title: India confined Pakistan navy to its ports during Operation Sindoor: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence minister Rajnath Singh revealed the Indian Navy's decisive role in Operation Sindoor, keeping Pakistan's navy confined to ports. Speaking at the Lucknow naval museum inauguration, he highlighted the operation's success following the Pahalgam terror attack. Singh also lauded India's growing defence manufacturing and export capabilities.

title: 70% drop in gold demand: Import duty hike from 6% to 15% rattles buyers
content: India's gold demand has plummeted by nearly 70% following a government increase in import duty from 6% to 15%. This, coupled with rising fuel and food prices, has significantly dampened consumer sentiment and spending on discretionary items like gold.

title: 'Not going to vacate': Rabri's 'evict by force' dare to CM Samrat over bungalow
content: The RJD leader reacted sharply when reporters questioned her about a recent order issued by the state's building construction department allotting the 10, Circular Road residence in Patna to Dairy and Fisheries Minister Nand Kishor Ram. "Yes, I can see that Samrat Choudhary, who has become the chief minister only recently, is quite excited. Let him get me evicted by force. I am not going to vacate the premises", said the indignant wife of RJD president Lalu Prasad.

title: India shows ‘cautious resilience’ despite Middle East conflict: DEA
content: India's economy shows 'cautious resilience' despite global headwinds like Middle East conflict and rising oil prices. While manufacturing and services remain strong, and forex reserves offer a buffer, inflation concerns are mounting due to wholesale price surges. A weaker monsoon could further challenge growth and rural demand.

title: Gujarat man sentenced to 10 years in prison without parole in minor trafficking case
content: A Nebraska hotel theft complaint unraveled a child trafficking ring involving Gujarati-origin operators. Kavankumar Patel received a 10-year sentence for sex trafficking minors. Investigations revealed hidden cameras and exploitation of victims, with Falguni Choudhary allegedly watching forced sex acts. Authorities continue to pursue traffickers.

title: How English-to-Hindi shift in many govt schools has Delhi parents, kids in a fix
content: Students in Delhi government schools are struggling as English-medium classes in Class XI are being discontinued. This forces them to choose between Hindi-medium or changing schools. Parents and teachers report negative impacts on academic performance and confidence. Education activists are calling for an inquiry into the matter to protect students' futures and ensure equal opportunities.

title: Why 8 in 10 patients turn to internet after doctor visits
content: A recent survey reveals a significant "patient confusion crisis" in Delhi-NCR, with nearly 80% of patients seeking online clarification post-doctor visits. Many feel rushed, lack guidance on next steps, and struggle to navigate hospitals, leading to increased costs and stress. Experts highlight overcrowded OPDs and weak guidance systems as key issues.

title: Is Indus Treaty deadlock hurting Pak? Karachi reels under chronic water shortage
content: Karachi faces a severe water crisis during peak summer, coinciding with Eid-ul-Adha. Jamaat-e-Islami Pakistan chief Hafiz Naeem ur Rehman has accused the ruling PPP of gross mismanagement and failure to address the city's chronic water shortages despite 18 years in power.  Nearly 70% of the city is reportedly experiencing extended supply disruptions, forcing residents to buy expensive private tankers.

title: Terror plot foiled: Nine nabbed for planning Delhi attack with ISI, underworld help
content: Delhi Police's Special Cell arrested nine terrorists linked to Pakistan's ISI and the Mumbai underworld, who were planning attacks on vital installations and security personnel in the capital. This operation follows a significant overhaul of Delhi Police's counter-terrorism framework, including the establishment of dedicated Counter-Terror Units in every police station to enhance intelligence gathering and threat identification.

title: Economy is in chaos due to NDA Government’s policies, says CPI leader Annie Raja
content: Centre has failed to handle the crisis properly, she says, alleging that the Prime Minister does not dare tell the truth to people

title: Two head of cattle found dumped on roadside in Ballari; BJP, Hindu organisations stage protest
content: Former Minister B. Sriramulu and MLA for Gangavathi Gali Janardhana Reddy visit protest site, demand swift action against culprits

title: Significant gains in women education, digital access, and economic agency but social constraints persist, NFHS-6 finds
content: While data show an uptick in school education and access to internet among women, deep-rooted gender norms continue to shape their life outcomes

title: Keep reminding yourself of responsibility towards contributing to nation’s progress: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi
content: Union Minister for Consumer Affairs, Food, Public Distribution, New and Renewable energy resources Pralhad Joshi has called on the students to keep reminding themselves of their responsibility towards contributing to the nation’s progress

title: Public health alert as influenza cases surge in Kerala
content: Viruses, such as Influenza B and A(H3N2 and H1N1), respiratory synctical virus (RSV) and COVID-19, are co-circulating in the community. Health department has advised people to maintain respiratory hygiene and etiquette and to use masks in public places as these viruses are highly transmissible.

title: Kerala SHRC directs RDO probe into ride collapse at amusement park
content: It seeks investigation into the reasons for the accident, whether the park received panchayat’s licence, details of those injured, and steps to prevent repeat of such accidents

title: Fake fee receipt scam unearthed in SV University
content: Manipulated fee receipts containing fake official seals an forged signature of employees have allegedly been generated and issued to students who paid fee towards examination certificates, as per the complaint lodged by the Registrar

title: Telangana DGP CV Anand calls for sweeping overhaul of police department, hints at restructuring Greyhounds and SID
content: Calling for a major shift in policing priorities, DGP Anand unveiled a reform agenda for the Telangana Police, proposing new specialised wings, a technology overhaul, stricter accountability measures and a review of anti-Naxal units.

title: New high-altitude plant species discovered in Western Ghats
content: The plant was found in high-altitude montane grasslands and shola forest margins of the Western Ghats, including regions such as Anamudi and Meesapulimala

title: Karnataka Information Commission disposed of 40,000 RTI cases in a year, says Commissioner
content: Information Commissioner B. Venkata Singh says strict action being taken against officials delaying information; ₹4.33 lakh penalty imposed in 101 days

title: Evict me by force, says Rabri Devi on her bungalow being allotted to a Bihar Minister
content: ‘Yes, I can see that Samrat Choudhary, who has become the Chief Minister only recently, is quite excited. Let him get me evicted by force. I am not going to vacate the premises,’ said Rabri

title: A.P. will lose if delimitation is done as per 2011 Census or latest data, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: Women should be given 33% quota, he asserts, adding that the TDP supports it as is evident from the resolution it passed in Mahanadu

title: Karnataka: Foundation stone-laying for KPS, mega PTM on school opening day cancelled owing to political climate
content: The Department of School Education and Literacy had organised the foundation stone-laying ceremony for 800 Karnataka Public Schools, along with the parents-teachers meeting, on June 1.

title: Collector directs officials to make Sri Sathya Sai district drug-free
content: Addressing a review meet of the district-level National Committee on Drug Law Enforcement, Shyam Prasad instructs all departments to leverage modern technology to track and prevent the movement of ganja and other narcotics

title: Police caution against sale of banned lottery tickets in Chittoor
content: Holding a counselling session for individuals previously involved in the sale of banned lottery, the DSP urges them to stay away from illegal activities and to reform and adopt lawful means of livelihood

title: Karnataka records gains in maternal and child health, but nutrition remains a concern: NFHS-6
content: The State records near-universal institutional deliveries, with 98.7% of births taking place in health facilities, up from 97% in NFHS-5

title: Attappady meet draws up tribal development charter
content: The meeting resulted in the preparation of a policy charter on land rights and tribal development, which will be submitted to the newly elected UDF government

title: Government launches ‘Operation Clean Sweep’ to  enhance awareness on sanitation
content: Commissioner and Director of Municipal Administration, along with District Collector and Panyam MLA, inaugurate desilting and sanitation works at the drainage canal near Ashwini Hospital, as well as at the Suddavagu stream

title: D.K. Shivakumar meets Governor Gehlot, stakes claim to form new govt in Karnataka
content: Earlier on Saturday, Mr. Shivakumar, who is also the State Congress president, was elected as the Leader of Congress Legislative Party (CLP); outgoing Chief Minister Siddaramaiah stepped down as the Leader of CLP after 17 years.

title: It was never a fair fight, but I am proud of myself: Vinesh after Asian Games selection trials defeat
content: The rancour between the federation and Vinesh had already given enough indications of an uneasy truce, mediated by the judiciary.

title: Only 15.3% children aged 6-23 months receive adequate diet: NFHS-6
content: Survey also shows decline in exclusive breastfeeding among children aged under six months, from 63.7% in NFHS-5 to 55.8% in NFHS-6

title: Kerala govt. to mandate creches in firms with over 50 employees: Minister Bindhu Krishna
content: Minister says steps will be taken to upgrade basic infrastructure at anganwadis and resolve concerns regarding food menu

title: Collector orders removal of debris from national highway worksites in Paravur
content: The directive was issued at a meeting of the District Development Committee after Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan’s representative raised the matter

title: TCS Nashik case: Hearing on Nida Khan’s bail plea deferred to June 12
content: Khan has been accused of targeting employees in a WhatsApp group, pressuring them to pray and eat non-vegetarian food

title: K.P. Dhanapalan, senior Congress leader and former Kerala MP, passes away
content: A prominent face of the Congress in Ernakulam, Mr. Dhanapalan was elected to the Lok Sabha from Chalakudy constituency in 2009

title: Karnataka: Amid hectic discussions, curiosity grows over new Cabinet formation
content: CM-designate D.K. Shivakumar is expected to visit New Delhi shortly for consultations with the high command on Cabinet formation, appointment of Deputy Chief Ministers, and selection of the Assembly speaker

title: Revival of Sabari rail project raises hopes in Central Travancore
content: The detailed field survey and preparation of drawings and related documents are set to resume along the 70-km stretch of the proposed alignment

title: Attack on ED officials in Kerala: bail plea of five accused rejected
content: The prosecution had contended that the charge of obstructing public servants in the discharge of their duties was applicable in the case; police have registered case against around 300 people suspected to have been involved in the attack

title: Arrangements in place ahead of Vice-President’s visit to Kottayam on May 31
content: He will inaugurate the 140th anniversary celebrations of Deepika, a vernacular daily and mouthpiece of the Catholic Church

title: Prevalence of diabetes continues to climb in southern States: NFHS
content: Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, and Tamil Nadu emerge as hotspots for overweight and obese adults, the National Family Health Survey-6 shows

title: "Rulers Became Killers": Mamata Banerjee vs BJP On Abhishek Banerjee Assault
content: The ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) said it wasn't involved in the assault on Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee.

title: Video: Abhishek Banerjee Assaulted During Visit To Party Worker's House
content: The crowds who confronted Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee also raised slogans including "chor, chor" against him

title: Trump Aide Warns US "More Than Capable" Of Restarting Iran War
content: The White House had signalled Trump was close to a decision on a potential deal, though Tehran denied there was a final agreement on ending the conflict.

title: How Iran's Hardliners Are Trying To Sabotage A Peace Deal With US
content: The hard-line opposition was on full display during a rally in Tehran on Friday, where supporters packed the streets carrying flags and calling for continued resistance.

title: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: DK Shivakumar Meets Governor, Stakes Claim As Chief Minister
content: Karnataka Politics Live Updates: A change in leadership in the three-year-old Karnataka government appears imminent, with Siddaramaiah having sought an appointment with Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot, reportedly to tender his resignation.

title: The Funding Route Pak's ISI-Backed Underworld Module In India Planned To Use
content: The Special Cell is continuing its probe to identify additional links, funding routes and potential recruits connected to the alleged module

title: Haryana Assures 1,000 Cusecs Of Water To Delhi As Yamuna Levels Fall
content: Despite Haryana's earlier release of additional water, supply disruptions and low pressure have continued in several parts of Delhi.

title: Surat Man Demands Euthanasia Over Alleged Harassment By Wife, In-Laws
content: Babubhai Patel has alleged that his wife attempted to murder his 12-year-old son by strangling him and smothering him with a roti.

title: Weather Today LIVE: Heavy Rain, Thunderstorm Hits Part Of Delhi
content: Weather Updates Today: Delhi remained under cloudy skies, logging its lowest maximum temperature in past 21 days. The IMD has forecast rain and thunderstorms across parts of north India, including Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, UP, Uttarakhand.

title: Video: Massive Dust Storm Plunges Rajasthan's Bikaner Into Near-Darkness
content: Markets were thrown into chaos as shopkeepers hurriedly pulled down shutters and pedestrians rushed to protect themselves from the storm.

title: 22 Dead As Truck Carrying Refugees From Pakistan Overturns In Afghanistan
content: Among the dead were 10 children and five women, and the injured were taken to hospitals in Nangarhar.

title: Journalist Assaulted, Media Office Vandalised In Assam's Silchar
content: The management of Barak Bani claimed that the journalist had previously faced intimidation over reports concerning alleged illegal activities, including betting and gambling operations.

title: Netanyahu Worried Trump May Strike A "Bad Interim Deal" With Iran: Report
content: For Israel, concerns remain that any emerging deal may leave out key issues such as Iran’s enriched uranium stockpile, its missile programme and support for regional proxy groups.

title: Vajiram And Ravi IAS Study Centre Fined Rs 7 Lakh For 'Misleading Ads'
content: The decision, said the CCPA, was taken to protect and promote the rights of consumers as a class and to ensure that no false or misleading advertisement is made

title: UP Residents To Pay 10% Fuel Surcharge On June Electricity Bills
content: The surcharge has been imposed to recover the additional expenditure incurred on power procurement and transmission costs in accordance with regulations framed by the Uttar Pradesh Electricity Regulatory Commission.

title: 9 With Links To Pak's ISI, Dawood Ibrahim Arrested, Were Planning Attacks In Delhi, Mumbai
content: The Delhi Police's Special Cell said the accused are residents of Delhi, Mumbai, and Punjab, and some are foreign nationals.

title: 'Kuki Militants Took Tactical Positions Along Route': Manipur Police On Ambush
content: The Manipur Police said the convoy "came under heavy attack in... a hill area by armed Kuki militants who had taken tactical positions along the route"

title: El Nino Likely To Result In Deficient Indian Monsoon, Raises Crop Concerns
content: Meteorologists are watching the Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD), another major climate phenomenon that occasionally offsets El Nino's drying influence on India.

title: DK Shivakumar meets Karnataka governor, stakes claim to form govt

title: Electricity bills in UP to rise 10% from June
content: When he's not chasing stories, you’ll likely find him exploring food lanes, indulging in mutton delicacies and sundaes. He is a journalist with a can-do spirit and a flair for compelling storytelling.  From railways and aviation to defence, infrastructure, social development and various other diverse beats, his reportage reflects depth.  His work has earned him the Times Scribe Award four times.
